atoms.source_snippet: pub max_extra_data_bytes: Option<usize>; known/default/builder specs use Some(MAXIMUM_EXTRA_DATA_SIZE); Genesis-derived specs use None; ChainSpecBuilder exposes max_extra_data_bytes(...)
atoms.semantic_claim: ChainSpec carries a per-chain header extra_data limit: canonical Ethereum specs and builder/default specs cap at MAXIMUM_EXTRA_DATA_SIZE, while genesis-derived Ritual chains can set None for unbounded CL snapshot extra_data, and the value is preserved through builders and accessors.

destinations.snippet: pub max_extra_data_bytes: Option<usize>; pub const fn max_extra_data_bytes(&self) -> Option<usize> { self.max_extra_data_bytes } ... max_extra_data_bytes: None
destinations.evidence: The port has the same per-chain Option<usize> field, known-chain/default MAXIMUM_EXTRA_DATA_SIZE defaults, genesis-derived None behavior, builder setter, and builder propagation, adapted to v2.2.0 ChainSpec generics.
Consensus validation consumes the ChainSpec limit, preserving the intended rejection path for capped chains and accepting unbounded chains.

destinations.snippet: let Some(max_size) = max_size else { return Ok(()) }; if extra_data_len > max_size { Err(ConsensusError::ExtraDataExceedsMax { len: extra_data_len }) }
destinations.evidence: The validation helper enforces Some(limit) and skips validation for None, matching the downstream chain-specific limit semantics.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Construct EthBeaconConsensus with ChainSpecBuilder::mainnet().max_extra_data_bytes(None) and a 64-byte header.extra_data; it must pass, while the same header under Some(MAXIMUM_EXTRA_DATA_SIZE) must fail with ExtraDataExceedsMax.
atoms.notes: Exact behavior is present after v2.2.0 type/name adaptation; consensus consumer evidence confirms the field is not dead plumbing.

atoms.source_snippet: pub base_block_reward: Option<u128>; let base_block_reward = genesis.config.base_block_reward; pub const fn base_block_reward(mut self, reward: Option<u128>) -> Self; fn base_block_reward(&self) -> Option<u128>
atoms.semantic_claim: ChainSpec stores an optional post-merge base block reward from genesis or builder configuration, defaults Ethereum specs to None, preserves the value through builder conversion, and exposes it through EthExecutorSpec for execution reward accounting.

destinations.snippet: pub base_block_reward: Option<u128>; let base_block_reward = genesis.config.base_block_reward; fn base_block_reward(&self) -> Option<u128> { self.base_block_reward }
destinations.evidence: The port keeps the optional reward field, genesis extraction, builder setter, conversion propagation, default None, and EthExecutorSpec exposure.
Genesis configuration has the base_block_reward field required by ChainSpec::from_genesis.
Execution consumes the ChainSpec-provided reward when no system-contract reward override is present.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build a genesis with config.base_block_reward = Some(2 ETH), convert to ChainSpec, execute or inspect EthExecutorSpec::base_block_reward, and verify the configured reward is returned; repeat with None to ensure Ethereum behavior remains no post-merge reward.
atoms.notes: Reward plumbing is present in the port and connected to the alloy-evm execution path.

atoms.source_snippet: pub fn effective_block_reward(&self, block_number: u64) -> Option<u128> { if self.is_paris_active_at_block(block_number) { self.base_block_reward } else { Some(self.base_block_reward_pre_merge(block_number)) } }
atoms.semantic_claim: ChainSpec computes effective base block rewards as configured custom rewards after Paris and standard 5/3/2 ETH rewards before Paris, ignoring custom post-merge reward configuration for pre-merge blocks.

destinations.evidence: The port carries the same ChainSpec effective reward helper and 5/3/2 ETH pre-merge schedule.
The dependency preserves the standard Ethereum pre/post-merge fallback calculation used when no custom reward is supplied.
Execution applies a custom reward when provided and otherwise falls back to standard pre-merge calculation, matching the downstream effective reward split.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Use a ChainSpec with Byzantium at block 100, Constantinople at block 200, Paris inactive before those blocks, and base_block_reward Some(1 ETH); effective_block_reward must return 5/3/2 ETH pre-merge and not the custom reward.
atoms.notes: The helper is present in the port; execution semantics are also represented in alloy-evm reward calculation.

atoms.semantic_claim: Genesis-derived Ritual/Summit deposit contracts use the Summit 7-field DepositEvent topic instead of the Ethereum mainnet 5-field deposit topic.

destinations.evidence: The destination port now defines the same Summit 7-field DepositEvent topic used by the downstream chainspec patch.
Genesis-derived ChainSpec deposit contracts now store SUMMIT_DEPOSIT_EVENT_TOPIC directly, and the regression test pins the exported ChainSpec deposit_contract.topic surface.

destinations.snippet: topic == Some(&DepositEvent::SIGNATURE_HASH) || topic == Some(&SUMMIT_DEPOSIT_SIGNATURE) ... if topic == Some(&SUMMIT_DEPOSIT_SIGNATURE) { ... accumulate_summit_deposit_from_event(&decoded_event, out); }
destinations.evidence: The destination EIP-6110 parser accepts and decodes the Summit 7-field deposit signature in the alloy-evm dependency, using the deposit contract address supplied by the spec.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Construct Genesis with deposit_contract_address, convert it to ChainSpec, and assert ChainSpec::deposit_contract().topic is SUMMIT_DEPOSIT_EVENT_TOPIC rather than the Ethereum mainnet topic.
atoms.notes: Implemented in the Reth port by restoring the chainspec-level Summit topic constant and using it in From<Genesis>; alloy-evm still independently accepts Summit deposit logs.

atoms.semantic_claim: Unit tests cover reward behavior across pre-merge fork boundaries, post-merge no-custom and custom rewards, genesis JSON baseBlockReward parsing, and ChainSpecBuilder Some/None reward propagation.

destinations.evidence: The destination spec tests now cover post-merge no-custom reward, post-merge custom reward, pre-merge fork-boundary rewards ignoring custom post-merge configuration, builder Some/None propagation, and genesis base_block_reward parsing.

destinations.snippet: assert_eq!(base_block_reward(&*MAINNET, block_number), expected_reward); ... assert_eq!(spec.base_block_reward, Some(reward)); assert_eq!(spec.effective_block_reward(0), Some(reward));
destinations.evidence: The port has partial reward coverage for standard base reward calculation and genesis-derived custom reward plus unbounded extra_data.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run cargo test -p reth-chainspec --lib and require the reward fixture tests to pass; deleting any tested branch should fail the targeted assertions.
atoms.notes: Missing adapted reward fixture coverage was added directly to crates/chainspec/src/spec.rs and passed in the reth-chainspec lib suite.

summary: Spec reward, extra_data, and Summit deposit-topic behavior are now ported with direct Reth-port evidence and focused regression tests.
notes: Formatting-only semicolon and rustfmt line-wrap changes were treated as noise. The previous file-0028 review items were resolved by restoring the chainspec-level Summit deposit topic for genesis-derived specs and adding adapted reward fixture coverage; verified with cargo test -p reth-chainspec --lib.
